meta.source: Illustrative relevance cases — synthetic examples of common hybrid-search ranking failure modes (constructed for documentation, not measurements from a specific vault).

categories.ranking-fusion: Correct note IS retrieved but ranked too low. Fix lever = fusion weights (alpha / recencyWeight / navTitleBoost) in ranker.ts DEFAULT_RANKING_CONFIG.
categories.recall-chunking: Correct note is NOT retrieved at all (absent from candidate pool). Fix lever = chunker.ts / candidate generation / index freshness.
categories.parser-operator: An inline query operator silently no-ops. Fix lever = query-parser.ts (parseQuery / compileMatcher).
categories.freshness: Note created same-day was not yet in the index at capture time. Fix lever = incremental indexing latency, not ranking.
categories.display: Relevance is correct; the rendered result is confusing. Fix lever = search-modal.ts / suggest.ts rendering, not ranking.

meta.scoring: rank is 1-based over the fused result list. A case PASSES when every path in `relevant` satisfies its assertion. Re-run after each config change to detect regressions.

cases.user_comment: The most recent 1x1 note is the relevant one here.
cases.evidence: Target note has dense 1.00 AND recency 0.98 yet ranks 3; two older notes win on higher bm25.
cases.root_cause_hypothesis: recencyWeight 0.09 too weak to break ties; alpha 0.7 lets bm25 from stale notes dominate a dense+recent winner.
cases.fix_lever: raise recencyWeight (ideally episodic-intent-gated) and/or raise alpha toward 0.9

cases.user_comment: This string matches against many notes — why is recency not the tie breaker?
cases.evidence: Among ~15 near-identical 'Alex 1x1 <date>' titles, the rank-1 note was 2026-05-19, not the newest. Dense score is driven by body content, so the title-match tie is broken by body, not recency.
cases.root_cause_hypothesis: For known-item title-collision queries, dense body similarity dominates and recencyWeight is too small to act as the intended tie-breaker.
cases.fix_lever: raise recencyWeight; consider title-exact-match boost so identical-title notes are tie-broken by recency not body

cases.evidence: Query explicitly contains '2026' yet a 2025 note ranks 2. The literal year token is not acting as a strong lexical/recency constraint.
cases.root_cause_hypothesis: Year token '2026' contributes weak BM25 (common across corpus) and there is no date-aware parsing of bare years in the query.
cases.fix_lever: consider parsing bare 4-digit years as a soft recency/date constraint in query-parser.ts; or raise recencyWeight

cases.user_comment: recent task out-ranking project home with exact name match
cases.evidence: The project HOME note (exact name match) is beaten by a recent task note (dense 1.00, recency 0.87). A controlled A/B raising navTitleBoost 0.025 -> 0.25 moved Project Atlas.md to rank 1 (score 1.01).
cases.root_cause_hypothesis: navTitleBoost too low to let an exact-title home note beat a dense+recent task; pageType not factored into ranking.
cases.fix_lever: raise navTitleBoost (caveat: widens the score distribution, complicating any future thresholding); consider pageType-aware boost for hub/home notes

observed.rank1: Notes/Tasks/Build up atlas clusters.md (pageType: task — should be excluded)

cases.evidence: Results were byte-identical to the unfiltered 'atlas project' query. The negated property filter parsed to a no-op; a pageType:task note remained at rank 1.
cases.root_cause_hypothesis: query-parser.ts does not handle negated bracket property filters '-[key:value]' (or the cleanedQuery stripped it without building a matcher).
cases.fix_lever: query-parser.ts: support negation on [key:value] filters; add a vitest case

cases.user_comment: why is file: not working here?
cases.evidence: 'file:' was expected to scope the match to filename/title. It had no effect — the task note still ranked 1, identical to no operator.
cases.root_cause_hypothesis: 'file:' operator is not implemented in query-parser.ts (parsed-and-dropped or treated as a literal term).
cases.fix_lever: query-parser.ts: implement file:/path scoping to the title/filename field; add a vitest case

cases.evidence: Clippings/GraphDB.md exists with a full body ('GraphDB is an in-memory graph database...') but was NOT retrieved. Its title frontmatter is 'The Graph Engine for AI Context' (no 'graphdb'); the term appears only in body + filename. created: same day as capture.
cases.root_cause_hypothesis: AMBIGUOUS — either (a) freshness: clipped after the last reindex so not yet a candidate, or (b) field mismatch: 'graphdb' is in body/filename but not the title field the ranker weights. Verify by reindexing then re-querying.
cases.fix_lever: if (a): incremental index latency; if (b): include filename as a searchable field / fall back to filename when title frontmatter lacks the term. Re-test after reindex to disambiguate.

cases.user_comment: I can't find snippets within notes. I'm looking for the metrics dashboard I believe is in my analytics home note.
cases.evidence: Analytics Home.md (11KB) contains the exact target — '[Quarterly metrics dashboard](https://analytics.example.com/...)' at line 23 — but never surfaced. The single 'dashboard' link is diluted across a large multi-chunk note; meeting notes outranked it.
cases.root_cause_hypothesis: Chunk granularity / rare-term dilution: the chunk holding the dashboard link scores low because the surrounding chunk is generic, and BM25 for the rare term is spread thin in a long note.
cases.fix_lever: chunker.ts granularity for long notes; consider surfacing best-matching chunk/section; verify BM25 term frequency handling for rare in-note terms

cases.user_comment: Relevance is fine, rendering needs work. We need to better call out the note title vs the section heading that matched.
cases.evidence: Ranking is CORRECT (rank 1, dense 1.0 bm25 1.0). This case is a passing-ranking baseline + a separate DISPLAY bug: the result UI does not distinguish note title from the matched section heading.
cases.root_cause_hypothesis: Not a ranking issue. Rendering in search-modal.ts conflates note title and matched heading.
cases.fix_lever: search-modal.ts / suggest.ts: visually separate note title from matched section heading. Keep this case to guard against ranking regressions on a clean known-item query.
